creator: ikostov@gmail.com
text:
Hi Tim,

I have modified the source code a little bit before using it in my project. As it turns out I have changed the line 


static const unsigned char base64_table[65] = ....

to 

static const unsigned char base64_encode_table[] =

which allocates indeed one byte less :). Sorry for the time lost :D 
However I don't find it sexy, to allocate 65 bytes and initialize only 64 of them and rely on the fact that static variables usually land in the .bss.
The code will work by initializing 64 elements and removing the " -1 " from the for loop.

Best regards,
Ivan
